items.title: Senior firefighter claims boss labelled her 'an overly emotional woman'

children.content: A former senior Northern Territory firefighter has claimed the service's deputy chief labelled her "an overly emotional woman" in a complaint to the territory's Anti-Discrimination Commission.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue uniform smiles for the camera.

items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

image.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

items.title: Mobile CT scanner visits region where 'at least one smoker in every house'

children.content: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

gallery.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'
A white tunnel medical machine, white walls inside with blue sheets and blue art pieces on the wall.
A white man in black t-shirt inside a large white tunnel scanning machine, with a nurse in red shirt and black vest standing.

thumbnail.altText: A white road train parked in a parking lot with starts coming out of its side, and shelter. Sign reads 'Heart of Australia'
A white tunnel medical machine, white walls inside with blue sheets and blue art pieces on the wall.
A white man in black t-shirt inside a large white tunnel scanning machine, with a nurse in red shirt and black vest standing.

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction

children.content: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

image.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

items.title: Second Mongols bikie to escape custody in the NT caught in eight minutes

children.content: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

image.altText: The side door of a NT Police car.

items.title: One CDU employee has been back paid $242k amid $4m stolen wages bill

children.content: Charles Darwin University (CDU) has been directed to back pay more than $4 million in wages after it was found to have underpaid more than 800 professional and academic staff members between 2016 and 2022.

image.altText: CDU sign

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

articles.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party

children.content: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

image.altText: Two women taking on radio into microphones.

articles.title: ASIO reveals new details on AUKUS espionage, warns of 'unprecedented threats'

children.content: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

image.altText: Mike Burgess, Head of ASIO, generic

articles.title: 'ISIS bride' barred from Australia issued permit to return

children.content: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

children.content: The government's signature budget bill, which includes changes to capital gains tax, negative gearing and a Working Australian Tax Offset (WATO), has passed federal parliament. Follow live.

items.title: Labor's CGT, negative gearing budget bill passes federal parliament

updates.title: Labor's signature budget measures pass federal parliament
Treasurer confirms changes to tax treatment of jointly owned assets in future legislation
Senate passes the government's signature budget bill

items.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party

children.content: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

image.altText: Two women taking on radio into microphones.

items.title: Hanson's Socceroos own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor

children.content: Pauline Hanson has chosen one of Australia's most popular national sporting brands and visible expressions of cultural diversity in her pitch for a "monoculture".

image.altText: Pauline Hanson stands in front of a blue backdrop. In front of her are two microphones.

images.altText: A car is smashed by the debris of a collapsed building
A building is collapsed into a mangled wreck.
A small group of people stand and talk in front of a white building with large cracks in its exterior at night.

children.content: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, local time, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.

image.altText: rubble and a collapsed building visited by emergency services

children.content: Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez has said at least 700 people have also been injured as emergency services work through the night to find those trapped under collapsed buildings. Follow live.

items.title: At least 32 people killed after back-to-back earthquakes rock Venezuela

updates.title: At least 32 people killed and hundreds injured
Four deaths reported in and around Caracas

items.title: 'Some cracks' appearing in jobs market, even as unemployment rate dips

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate slipped to 4.4 per cent in May, down from 4.5 per cent in April, in seasonally adjusted terms.

image.altText: Boxing Day sales Pitt Street mall Sydney

items.title: Tribute to 'respected' WA pilot after fatal flight in plane he built himself

children.content: A friend of 76-year-old pilot Malcolm Vivian remembers him as a "beautiful man" and "special aircraft builder" after his death in a light plane crash near Northam, and says he does not think pilot error was to blame.

image.altText: Two men piloting a light aircraft wearing headsets

items.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

articles.title: What today's results mean for each World Cup team in action

children.content: The path to the World Cup knockouts is more complicated than ever. Here is what Thursday's results mean for the path ahead.

image.altText: A collage of players reacting to results.

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

image.altText: Jacob Italiano #4 of Australia controls the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026

articles.title: Traffic cones on statues and a sea of orange: The traditions taking over the World Cup

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

image.altText: Norway fans sitting in Times Square doing a Viking row

gallery.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

items.title: Olympian arrested at reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says

children.content: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

image.altText: People gather near the reflecting pool, including a person waving a large American flag.

items.title: The potentially deadly substance being delivered to Aussie doors at all hours

children.content: The importation of nitrous oxide has exploded in the last decade. Nangs are increasingly being used by some to chase a high and causing significant damage.

image.altText: A photo of a man sits atop a grave which is covered in flowers.

items.title: Swimming group takes 'chilling with friends' to another level

children.content: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

gallery.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

items.title: Ghost nets transformed into art to highlight pollution's 'devastating' impact

children.content: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

image.altText: A First Nations man and woman hold a large fish sculpture made from netting

items.title: When can gambling become an addiction? Here are the warning signs

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

items.title: As F1 heads to Austria the final result of Monaco goes to the courts

children.content: As F1 descends on the scenic Styrian Mountains for this weekend's Austrian Grand Prix, two teams are in the process of reversing the result in Monaco.

items.title: ASX slips while gold futures drop below $US4,000 level

items.title: Australians' tricks for dealing with heatwaves that Europeans yet to learn

children.content: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

image.altText: Four people playing in water near the Eiffel Tower

items.title: As Ebola spreads, Australia faces rise in 'biblical' disease risks

children.content: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

image.altText: illustration of 4 people in a web of dots representing how viruses spread

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: France and Vanuatu clash over remote Pacific island where 'god resides'

children.content: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

gallery.altText: A man takes a photo of a volcanic island from the front of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: A man takes a photo of a volcanic island from the front of a boat.

items.title: Green back on happy hunting ground with chance to end golf-major drought

children.content: Australian Hannah Green is once again in sparkling form as she returns to the scene of her maiden major win in 2019 with hopes of ending ending her seven-year major drought.

image.altText: Hannah Green stands with her arms folded at Hazletine National Golf Club.

items.title: Brisbane Broncos warn of scam offering fake mascot auditions

children.content: A scam is circulating online falsely offering fans a chance to audition to be the Brisbane Broncos' mascot in exchange for payment.

image.altText: The Brisbane Broncos mascot points.

items.title: Understanding of harmful algal blooms still murky, disaster survey finds

children.content: Most Australians remain uncertain about the cause of harmful algal blooms and are more worried about disasters such as bushfires and flooding.

image.altText: Piles of dead fish on a beach at sunset

items.title: Brisbane teen acquitted of planning terror attack targeting Peter Dutton

children.content: During the trial, the prosecution alleged when he was 16, the teen planned and prepared to set off an improvised explosive device at a Queensland Labour Day event in May 2024.

image.altText: Peter Dutton in a room during the campaign.

items.title: Wildlife carers did not expect to be 'ground zero' for third H5 bird flu case

children.content: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.

items.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

items.title: The alternative burial method that could soon come to Australia

children.content: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

image.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

thumbnail.altText: A dark coloured feral pig in bushland

items.title: Residents in regional Victoria hire security patrols over crime fears

children.content: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

image.altText: A car at night with flashing lights.

items.title: 86-year-old great-grandmother loves to 'thrash her ute' doing burnouts

children.content: Lorraine "Nan" Tuckett loves nothing more than letting the back end of her ute slide and smelling the rubber burn. She travels around Australia doing burnouts.

image.altText: An older woman waves an orange flag out the side of a black ute with smoke coming out the back of it
